exemplar.title: Is our most revered knowledge more fragile than we assume it to be? Discuss with reference to the arts and one other area of knowledge.

detailedExemplarData.title: Is our most revered knowledge more fragile than we assume it to be? Discuss with reference to the arts and one other area of knowledge.

strengths: Balanced recognition of the essayâs consistent focus on the prescribed title and use of relevant real-world examples.
Clear awareness of contrasting perspectives, particularly the provisional nature of scientific knowledge versus the subjectivity of the arts.
Coherent structure overall, with logical progression from the introduction through to the conclusion.
Effective engagement with the prescribed title across multiple areas of knowledge.
weaknesses: Analysis of examples remains largely descriptive; depth of critical examination and exploration of implications is limited.
Core TOK concepts (justification, knowledge frameworks, paradigm shifts) are invoked only implicitly rather than being explicitly developed.
Links between real-world examples and theoretical TOK terminology are under-developed, reducing theoretical rigor.
Transitions between sections (especially shifting from arts to natural sciences) are abrupt and sometimes lack clear signposting.

annotations.comment: The prescribed title is clearly stated at the outset, providing precise focus for the essayâs exploration of fragility in revered knowledge.
The example of Chinese opera is descriptive but unexamined; the student should analyze specific elements (e.g., tonal structures or audience reception) to illustrate fragility.
Integrate TOK vocabulary such as âknowledge frameworkâ or âperspectiveâ to strengthen your link between example and core concepts.
Opening with Russellâs memorable quote effectively anchors the discussion and immediately engages with the prescribed question about scrutinizing knowledgeâs imperfections.
The term ârevered knowledgeâ is introduced without clear operational definition; the student should refine this concept to clarify the scope of analysis.
Consider explicitly introducing core TOK concepts such as justification and communities of knowers to deepen the theoretical foundation of your argument.
The citation of Dove-development.net is unexamined; the student should assess the credibility and relevance of online sources to strengthen justification.
Introduce Thomas Kuhnâs concept of a âparadigm shiftâ to ground your discussion of scientific revolutions in TOK theory.
The historical example of Newtonian physics giving way to Einsteinâs relativity powerfully illustrates the provisional nature of scientific knowledge.
The counterclaim could engage with Imre Lakatosâs idea of research programmes to deepen the evaluation of scientific progression.
Noting that Newtonâs laws remain useful in practical contexts demonstrates awareness of implications and nuances of paradigm shifts.
The conclusion reiterates earlier points but stops short of synthesising implications across areas of knowledge; aim for a more integrative final reflection.
Reflect on how the fragility of revered knowledge informs responsible citizenship and decision-making to connect back to TOKâs real-world significance.
The thesis statement lacks a clear preview of the main comparative points between the arts and natural sciences; a concise roadmap would improve structural clarity.
Recognizing subjectivity in the arts as a source of fragility is appropriate and aligns well with the prescribed questionâs focus on cultural interpretation.
Using a cross-cultural example demonstrates awareness of different perspectives and supports the claim about the artsâ vulnerability.
Describing Chinese operaâs music as âbizarreâ introduces bias; adopt neutral language to maintain an objective tone.
The discussion of cultural and temporal context effectively illustrates how the artsâ meaning shifts, supporting the fragility argument.
The pivot to artsâ resilience lacks a clear signposting phrase; adding a transition such as âHoweverâ would improve coherence.
The paragraph ends abruptly (ââ¦this playâ); ensure full development of the thought and complete sentences for academic rigor.
Consider including an additional example of resilience in the arts, such as a modern work that transcends cultural shifts, for deeper comparison.
The nuanced conclusion that arts knowledge can be both fragile and resilient demonstrates depth of thought and balanced evaluation.
The transition from the arts to natural sciences is abrupt; include a bridging sentence to signal change of area of knowledge explicitly.
Reliance on a non-academic site (Texasgateway.org) is unqualified; justify or replace with peer-reviewed sources to meet TOK standards.
The discussion of Newtonian mechanicsâ ongoing utility in engineering contexts shows a balanced understanding of fragility versus practicality.
The phrase âresistance of scientific knowledgeâ is unclearâspecify who or what resists change and how this operates within the scientific community.
Discuss Karl Popperâs notion of falsifiability to reinforce your argument about why scientific theories remain provisional.
The use of a first-person statement (âI believeâ) weakens academic tone; maintain formal third-person voice for consistency.
The closing remark on continual questioning captures the essence of TOKâs emphasis on ongoing inquiry and demonstrates thoughtful personal engagement.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content:
The essay does not
reach a standard
described by levels
1â5 or is not a
response to one of
the prescribed titles
on the list for the
current session.
The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

The discussion
is focused on
the title and is
developed with
some links to
areas of
knowledge.
Arguments are
offered and are
supported by
examples.
There is some
awareness of
different points
of view.

The discussion is focused on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and supported by examples. There is awareness and some evaluation of different points of view.

The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
